polls.text: Are you interested in seeing this work progress in OPSAWG?
Is there interest in adopting this draft in OPSAWG?

chat.author: Mahesh Jethanandani
chat.text: I did have a question on the GPON draft. Did we resolve all 17 issues that Paul brought up as part of Expert Review.

chat.author: Benoît Claise
chat.text: @Mahesh, not yet. So WGLC would be done after.

chat.author: Mahesh Jethanandani
chat.text: Makes sense. Thanks.

chat.author: Reshad Rahman
chat.text: Would it help if router-id is used now but embedded in a choice statement

chat.author: Reshad Rahman
chat.text: Disclaimer: haven't checked the document recently

chat.author: Joe Clarke
chat.text: ne-id is a key in the list.  It might make sense to pick some ID that can be unique across a domain regardless of ne type to ensure you have a way to uniquely correlate results.

chat.author: Qin Wu
chat.text: @Reshad, @Joe, @Jeff Thanks for your inputs.

chat.author: Qin Wu
chat.text: @Thomas, it will be useful to clarify why we can not use IPFIX exported data fields for debugging purpose? or we will not use any  IPFIX fields for debugging

chat.author: Joe Clarke
chat.text: @Rob and @Per please post those comments on Provenance to the list.  And thanks for the review!

chat.author: Benoît Claise
chat.text: And @Mahesh too for the (tooling?) error you were referring to.

chat.author: Dan Voyer
chat.text: option A, when easy and known, people just follow

chat.author: James Cumming
chat.text: This feels like you need a poll on this

chat.author: Thomas Graf
chat.text: @Mahesh, regarding draft-ietf-opsawg-ipfix-gpon-gema. The comments from Paul will be addressed in the next document revision.

chat.author: Joe Clarke
chat.text: @James, thought about that, but it's hard to do a four-way poll with the show of hands tool.

chat.author: James Cumming
chat.text: IETF retaining ownership to the data is important - So I would vote for B

chat.author: James Cumming
Benoît Claise
chat.text: "That's a shame - Sounds like a feature request" Meetecho is monitoring this channel :-)

chat.author: Joe Clarke
chat.text: Would be nice to be able to create a custom poll with flexible number of options...

chat.author: Dhruv Dhody
chat.text: Does the IETF tools team does some backup for WG GitHub’s?

chat.author: Joe Clarke
chat.text: Good question.  I don't know.  I can check with Robert.

chat.author: Joe Clarke
chat.text: As a contributor, VELOCE is an experiment.  I'm of the thinking we use GitHub for now, prove the experiment, and then look to see if we need to choose something else for the future.  Ultimately, it could be work is done in GH and artifacts live elsewhere in the long-run.

chat.author: Joe Clarke
chat.text: @Dhruv Robert says, "no. We are supposed to, and did briefly a few years ago, but the mechanism hasn’t worked in some time. I’ve started a conversation just today about starting to do so again."

chat.author: Dan Voyer
chat.text: ha, nice - no backup

chat.author: Mahesh Jethanandani
chat.text: On the YANG provenance draft, I notice the datatracker mentions 4 errors at https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-ietf-opsawg-yang-provenance/#. I normally do not pay heed to the errors, but in this case, it is worth checking whether the errors being reported are true errors or just false positives.

chat.author: Chongfeng Xie
chat.text: One of the  motivations of this draft is to make the data communication more secure by using QUIC, however,  in the network of big operators, the telemetry data is transfered by a specific VPN network, not on public Internet, in this case, data transfer with plan-text is not big secure risk,  so I doubt the real value of moving Telemetry to QUIC transport with great upgrade cost.

chat.author: Dan Voyer
chat.text: "a specific VPN network" like an IPSEC encryption or VPN as L3VPN ?! :sunglasses:

chat.author: Holger Keller
chat.text: @chongfeng, a VPN is not secure by default, and when the data leaves the network and enters the IT-Area? how is a collector ensuring, that the messages he gets are from a "valid" sender?

chat.author: Ebben Aries
chat.text: re: VELOCE: +1 on James and others' comment on data ownership being a main objective but also we want to consider git scm today, something else tomorrow, front-ends and review tooling can change so being as agnostic as possible concentrating on modern revision control, history, data retention.  GitHub imo is perfectly fine for quick-and-dirty immediate but +1 for self-hosted/full-control.

chat.author: Mahesh Jethanandani
chat.text: Have a request. For those folks who came to the mike to discuss the VELOCE draft, please add/verify your comments in the meeting notes. I seem to be missing a few of them.

chat.author: Ebben Aries
chat.text: @chongfeng - agree alot of env. handle the security considerations by various other means today but there are growing needs and mandates for transport level encryption and common/consolidated transports - in today's non-IETF defined protocols, TLS is heavily in used even in those "internal-only" networks

chat.author: Chongfeng Xie
chat.text: @Dan Voyer, L3VPN.

chat.author: Qin Wu
chat.text: @Benoit support both datagram frame and stream frame seems to mean we separate control plane from data plane, do we ask datagram frame and stream datagram to share the same connection  or sit in different connection? also do we have implementation as reference?

chat.author: Ebben Aries
chat.text: re: "great upgrade cost" is real - I'd envision this as an evolution adopted over a long time, nowhere near immediate

chat.author: Reshad Rahman
chat.text: Adding to what @Ebben said, people who don't want/need to migrate to XoverQUIC, don't have to.

chat.author: Holger Keller
chat.text: @ebben if you already have the protocol activated, yes on migration. we have strong security requirements for transport, the collector and the message-broker (well, only auth there, no encryption). If there is no auth or security, we have to provide mechanisms inside the collector to come around that, which means a lot of effort -  QUIC solves that immediately.

chat.author: Reshad Rahman
chat.text: OOC was the document presented in QUIC?

chat.author: Joe Clarke
chat.text: @Reshad while I don't know offhand, we would cross-post the CfA to QUIC for feedback.

chat.author: Mahesh Jethanandani
